title: tiglium officinale
intro: Tiglium officinale, also known as the common foxglove, is a biennial plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its cardiac glycoside compounds, which have been used to treat conditions such as heart failure and atrial fibrillation. The health benefits of Tiglium officinale include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve heart function, and reduce the risk of heart failure. Tiglium officinale is often prepared as a tincture, capsule, or infusion for medicinal use, although it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
